mechanism_narrative: A consistently low unemployment rate indicates a strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment generally correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative on average during periods of UNRATE below 5% over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If, when the trigger condition is met, the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and housing starts are increasing, it signals an accommodative monetary policy supporting a recovering housing market, which boosts overall economic activity and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2% and HOUST growth is above 10%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly negative or show no significant positive trend.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during a period of economic contraction signals weakening consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate further economic slowdown.
failure_condition: If, during identified periods of economic contraction, a significant rise in UNRATE is followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and strong supply-side capacity, which supports overall economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output is a key economic indicator
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the Consumer Price Index signals rising inflation, which can erode corporate profit margins and lead to tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation can lead to rate hikes
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 3%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive on average over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is growing strongly alongside robust retail sales, it indicates a powerful combination of job creation and consumer spending, which is highly supportive of corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% and RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If, during periods where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show strong growth, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising Federal Funds Rate during a period of economic slowdown indicates restrictive monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs and dampens economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods of high and rising FEDFUNDS, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is below 0.05.
